setup.overview:
Finding Lew — The Finder's Price

Level: 1 | Beat: 5 | Location: Stairwatch, outside the chandler's shop

The PCs have tracked down Llewyellen “Lew” Tosferd — a one-eyed finder-for-hire with a reputation for expensive miracles. They need him to locate Kaelen, the missing Ember-Keeper. The problem: Lew doesn't work for strangers, doesn't work for free, and has his own troubles in Stairwatch that need handling first.

Lew is friendly, casual, and already three steps ahead. He knows about the Konungs-ferð, suspects these aren't ordinary travelers, and will listen to their pitch before naming his price.

GM Notes: Playing Lew

Lew is not an obstacle — he's a transaction. He genuinely wants to help, the way a merchant genuinely wants to sell you something. He's warm, funny, and utterly without sentiment about money.

  • He asks questions constantly — genuine curiosity and professional assessment.
  • He never lies, but tells you exactly as much as he's decided to tell you.
  • He refers to his finding-gift obliquely: “I have a knack,” “things turn up.”
  • He is never rude. He is always expensive.

What Lew Wants

If the PCs succeed, Lew names his price — two things: (1) a favor to be named later, and (2) help resolving a local problem before he leaves (the Missing Miners or the Debt Collector).

outcomes.success:
Success

Lew agrees to find Kaelen. He names his price — a favor and one local task — and then shakes hands. “Fair trade,” he says. “Now. Let me tell you about the mine.”

Lew joins the party as a guide; his amber eye warms and pulls southeast, toward the Ashen Woods.
outcomes.partialSuccess:
Partial Success

Lew agrees, but his terms are harder. He demands two local tasks completed before departure, and the favor he extracts is more specific — something the PCs may later regret promising. He's pleasant about it. That makes it worse.
outcomes.failure:
Failure

Lew declines. Politely, warmly, and absolutely. “I like you. That's why I'm saying no — you're not ready to make the promises this job requires.” He goes back to his whittling.

The PCs must find another leverage point (completing the local tasks first, or returning with a concrete offer) to re-open the negotiation.

participants.notesGM: One-eyed finder-for-hire. The right eye is an amber gem given by Gragnir — it pulls toward lost things. Lew is warm, transactional, and professionally fearless. He will negotiate in good faith but always names his price.
